unit.comment: // Copyright Catch2 Authors
// Distributed under the Boost Software License, Version 1.0.
// (See accompanying file LICENSE.txt or copy at
// https://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt)
// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSL-1.0
// Setup for #1403 -- look for global overloads of operator << for classes
// in a different namespace.
// Comparison operators can return non-booleans.
// This is unusual, but should be supported.

function.name: templated_tests

unit.comment: // Note that because -~GCC~-, this warning cannot be silenced temporarily, by pushing diagnostic stack...
// Luckily it is firing in test files and thus can be silenced for the whole file, without losing much.
// This is a minimal example for an issue we have found in 1.7.0
// Changes to REQUIRE_THROWS_AS made it stop working in a template in
// an unfixable way (as long as C++03 compatibility is being kept).
// To prevent these from happening in the future, this needs to compile
// Test containing example where original stream insertable check breaks compilation
// Comparison operators can return non-booleans.
// This is unusual, but should be supported.
// unsigned array
// The function pointer comparison below triggers warning because of
// calling conventions

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("#925: comparing function pointer to function address failed to compile", "[!nonportable]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#1319: Sections can have description (even if it is not saved", "[compilation]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("Assertion macros support bit operators and bool conversions", "[compilation][bitops]")

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("Immovable types are supported in basic assertions", "[compilation][.approvals]")

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("ADL universal operators don't hijack expression deconstruction", "[compilation][.approvals]")

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#2555 - types that can only be compared with 0 literal implemented as pointer conversion are supported", "[compilation][approvals]" )

unit.comment: // These tests require `consteval` to propagate through `constexpr` calls
// which is a late DR against C++20.
// Can't have internal linkage to avoid warnings
// namespace

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#2555 - types that can only be compared with 0 literal implemented as consteval check are supported", "[compilation][approvals]" )

unit.comment: // We check all comparison ops to test, even though orderings, the primary
// motivation for this functionality, only have self-comparison (and thus
// have the ambiguity issue) for `==` and `!=`.

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "Comparing const instances of type registered with capture_by_value", "[regression][approvals][compilation]" )

unit.comment: // C++20 consteval

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("#2571 - tests compile types that have multiple implicit constructors from lit 0", "[compilation][approvals]")

unit.comment: // This test does not test all the related codepaths, but it is the original
// reproducer

diff:insert.comment: // Reproduce issue with yaml-cpp iterators, where the `const_iterator`
// for Node type has `const T` as the value_type. This is wrong for
// multitude of reasons, but there might be other libraries in the wild
// that share this issue, and the workaround needed to support
// `from_range(iter, iter)` helper with those libraries is easy enough.
